annotations.label: Measurable function (informal)

anchor.snippet: a measurable function is a function between the underlying sets of two measurable spaces that preserves the structure of the spaces

annotations.note: `Measurable` formalizes the structure-preserving function between measurable spaces.

annotations.label: Measurable function (formal)

anchor.snippet: is said to be measurable if for every

annotations.note: `Measurable f` is defined as: the preimage of every measurable set is measurable.

annotations.label: σ-algebra generated by f

anchor.snippet: is the σ-algebra generated by f

annotations.note: `MeasurableSpace.comap f m` is the σ-algebra on the domain generated by preimages under `f` of measurable sets in the codomain.

annotations.label: Borel function

anchor.snippet: are Borel spaces , a measurable function

annotations.note: A Borel function in Mathlib is just `Measurable f` between spaces equipped with `BorelSpace`; there is no separate named definition.

annotations.label: Continuous implies Borel

anchor.snippet: Continuous functions are Borel functions but not all Borel functions are continuous

annotations.note: `Continuous.measurable` states that a continuous function between Borel-measurable spaces is measurable.

annotations.label: Luzin's theorem

anchor.snippet: a measurable function is nearly a continuous function

annotations.note: No Lusin/Luzin theorem (measurable function agrees with a continuous function off a small-measure set) was found in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: it is called a Borel section

annotations.label: Lebesgue measurable function

anchor.snippet: A Lebesgue measurable function is a measurable function

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dedicated `LebesgueMeasurable`; Lebesgue measurability is captured via `Measurable` w.r.t. the Lebesgue σ-algebra or via `NullMeasurable` (measurable up to a null set).

annotations.label: Equivalent characterizations of Lebesgue measurability

anchor.snippet: is Lebesgue measurable if and only if

annotations.note: Mathlib relates `NullMeasurable` and `Measurable` under completeness, but a packaged equivalence of all the classical Lebesgue characterizations is not provided.

annotations.label: Classes of Lebesgue measurable functions

anchor.snippet: Continuous functions, monotone functions, step functions, semicontinuous functions, Riemann-integrable functions, and functions of bounded variation are all Lebesgue measurable

annotations.note: Individual classes (continuous, monotone, etc.) have measurability lemmas (`Continuous.measurable`, `Monotone.measurable`, ...), but no single aggregated statement exists.

annotations.label: Complex measurability via real and imaginary parts

anchor.snippet: is measurable if and only if the real and imaginary parts are measurable

annotations.note: `Complex.measurable_re` and `Complex.measurable_im` give the forward direction, but no explicit `Measurable f ↔ Measurable re∘f ∧ Measurable im∘f` lemma was found.

annotations.label: Sum and product of measurable functions

anchor.snippet: The sum and product of two complex-valued measurable functions are measurable

annotations.note: `Measurable.add` and `Measurable.mul` formalize closure of measurability under sums and products.

annotations.label: Quotient of measurable functions

anchor.snippet: So is the quotient, so long as there is no division by zero

annotations.note: `Measurable.div` states the quotient of two measurable functions is measurable (in Mathlib's convention `x/0 = 0`).

annotations.label: Composition of measurable functions

anchor.snippet: are measurable functions, then so is their composition

annotations.note: `Measurable.comp` formalizes closure of measurability under composition.

annotations.label: Composition of Lebesgue-measurable functions need not be Lebesgue-measurable

anchor.snippet: two Lebesgue-measurable functions may be constructed in such a way as to make their composition non-Lebesgue-measurable

annotations.note: No Mathlib construction of two Lebesgue-measurable functions whose composition fails to be Lebesgue-measurable was located.

annotations.label: Sup, inf, limsup, liminf are measurable

anchor.snippet: supremum , infimum , limit superior , and limit inferior of a sequence (viz., countably many) of real-valued measurable functions are all measurable

annotations.label: Pointwise limit of measurable functions is measurable

anchor.snippet: The pointwise limit of a sequence of measurable functions

annotations.note: `measurable_of_tendsto_metrizable` states that the pointwise limit of a sequence of measurable functions (into a metrizable space) is measurable.

annotations.label: Non-measurable indicator function

anchor.snippet: one can construct a non-measurable indicator function

annotations.note: No explicit `not Measurable` indicator construction (e.g. on a Vitali set) was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-constant function on trivial σ-algebra is non-measurable
annotations.note: No lemma stating that on the trivial σ-algebra a non-constant function is not measurable was located.
